seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: composite: skin balanced max-cut bisection as two-AGV fleet zoning, and plant the heavy congestion weight on a hidden balanced ground-truth cut that is decorrelated from the checker's alternating index baseline, so the reference partition is far above the baseline while light intra/noise conflicts create a genuine balance-vs-cut tradeoff.
title: Fleet Zoning: Two-AGV Congestion Split
strategies: alternating baseline: label cell i by i mod 2 (balanced but uncorrelated with the good cut)
sequential greedy: assign each cell to the fleet that maximizes incremental cut weight against already-placed cells, respecting fleet-size caps
pairwise-swap local search: from a balanced start, repeatedly apply the best cross-fleet cell swap that increases the cut, with randomized multi-restart
planted-recovery heuristics: exploit heavy-edge community structure to approximately recover the balanced ground-truth partition
